text.title: shadcn/ui Architecture and Composition
text.summary: Directory structure, primitive vs composition pattern, CVA variant management, and the copy-not-install model.

rule.statement: shadcn/ui components are copied into `components/ui/` via `npx shadcn@latest add`, not installed as an npm package. You own the source.
rule.rationale: Owning the source means agents can edit components directly, you control bug fixes, and there is no vendor lock-in. The trade-off is that upstream fixes must be pulled explicitly via `npx shadcn diff`.

rule.statement: Never modify files in `components/ui/` directly. Extend by composing primitives in `components/features/` or `components/forms/`.
rule.rationale: Direct edits to `ui/` create a local fork. When you run `npx shadcn add button` again, your edits are overwritten. All customization goes into composition layers above the primitives.

example.code: src/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ components/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ui/ # shadcn primitives вЂ” treat as vendored в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ button.tsx в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ card.tsx в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ dialog.tsx в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ forms/ # Form-specific compositions в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ CreateUserForm.tsx в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ features/ # Feature-level compositions в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ Dashboard/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Dashboard.tsx в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ MetricsCard.tsx в”њв”Ђв”Ђ lib/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ utils.ts # cn() helper в””в”Ђв”Ђ styles/ в””в”Ђв”Ђ globals.css # CSS variables, @tailwind directives

rule.statement: Use CVA (class-variance-authority) for all variant-bearing components. Declare `compoundVariants` for cross-cutting state combinations instead of repeating base classes.
rule.rationale: Without CVA, variants accumulate as ad-hoc className conditions that conflict and produce unexpected results. CVA makes variants explicit, typed, and composable.
